tax: Placed at family rank, Evaporite Crystalkin is defined by composition and formation mechanics, not by colour, size, or danger: a soft evaporite-lattice body fed by sulphate or salt seep. A new genus belongs here only if it shows a soft evaporite body with its own cleavage habit.
ap: The grouping is recognised by a satin blade-sheaf or salt cluster with a soft sheen. A field surveyor confirms membership from at least one persistent marker — silky fibre-heaps and parallel scratch-fans — which survives even when the body disperses.
eco: Evaporite Crystalkin occupies gypsum curtains and salt-evaporite seeps. Persistence is elemental rather than reproductive: members form where a sulphate seep grows a fibrous mass and recur with the evaporite seep refeeds it, sustaining themselves on sulphate or salt supersaturation.
beh: Across the grouping, behaviour is the disturbance-response of the medium: poses as a curtain, sweeps cleaving blades to touch. Roles, moods, and summoning circumstances are recorded on the entries below, not treated as new taxa.
traitsText: Shared characters: soft cleaving body and crushing immunity. These are grouping-level traits only; specific resistances, abilities, and field signs belong to each genus and species beneath this node.
abilities: Grouping-level abilities are the broad capacities implied by the shared body: self-sharpening blades. Named, bounded powers are assigned to the species that actually possess them.
bg: In the Throat, Evaporite Crystalkin matters because mistaken for harmless curtain selenite. It lets survey reports sort soft cleaving hazards from hard ringing ones without collapsing distinct hazards into one vague label.
g: Evaporite Crystalkin is the family-rank grouping of soft sulphate and salt crystalline elementals within the True Elementals. Its members share a soft, often fibrous evaporite body that cleaves rather than shatters, and divide below into genus groups by the evaporite mineral — fibrous gypsum among them. Soft, silent, and self-sharpening, the opposite of the Quartz-Kin.
